projectName: SWLS – Semantic Web Language Server for VS Code
projectDescription: A VS Code extension that brings IDE-quality support for Semantic Web languages — Turtle, TriG, JSON-LD, and SPARQL — via a language server with autocompletion, diagnostics, formatting, and SHACL validation.
keyFeatures: Autocompletion for prefixes, properties, and classes in Turtle, TriG, JSON-LD, and SPARQL
Diagnostics for syntax errors, undefined prefixes, and SHACL shape violations
Document formatting for Turtle and JSON-LD
Hover information showing labels, descriptions, and class info for IRIs
Rename support for terms local to the current file
Full semantic token highlighting for all supported languages
WASM fallback — works immediately without any native binary setup
Automatic update checking and installation from GitHub releases
Configurable property completion modes: none, loose, and strict
publicApiSurface: swls.command — path to swls native binary
swls.checkUpdate — check GitHub for new releases on startup
swls.log — log level: error, warn, info, debug, trace
swls.ontologies — extra ontology URLs to load
swls.shapes — extra SHACL shape URLs to load
swls.prefixDisabled — prefix.cc prefixes to hide from completions
swls.completion.mode — property completion mode: none, loose, strict
swls.completion.strict — namespace prefixes that require domain matching
swls.completion.except — namespace prefixes always suggested regardless of domain
